title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. McGillicuddy, a traveler from the north of England, boards a 4:50 PM train at Platform 3 for a journey toward Blachampton and other destinations. After a porter delivers her luggage to a spacious, nearly empty first-class carriage, she falls asleep for approximately thirty-five minutes. During her journey through a dark, foggy December landscape, her train passes another train traveling in the same direction.

As the two trains run side by side, Mrs. McGillicuddy observes the first-class carriage of the passing train through the window. She witnesses a man standing with his back to the window, brutally strangling a woman until her face turns black and purple and she goes limp. After the trains diverge, a stunned Mrs. McGillicuddy attempts to report the murder to a conductor who enters her compartment to check her ticket.

The conductor is skeptical of her claims, suggesting that she may have been dreaming or influenced by an exciting story in the magazine she was reading, which depicts a similar scene of violence. Mrs. McGillicuddy insists she was wide awake and witnessed the crime with her own eyes. The conductor, appearing to believe she is merely overzealous, promises to report the incident. Before the train arrives at Blachampton, he records her name and her home address in Scotland to facilitate a report.
